privacy_classes.plain_description: Information that can be public without identifying a private learner record.
privacy_classes.handling_notes: Still review tenant policy before publishing.

privacy_classes.plain_description: Basic school directory information such as a class name or school name.
privacy_classes.handling_notes: Usually visible only to authorized users in the tenant.

privacy_classes.plain_description: Student education record data such as enrollment, grade, result, or progress.
privacy_classes.handling_notes: Protect under student-data policy and tenant access rules.

privacy_classes.plain_description: Data that could create extra risk if disclosed, such as accommodations or sensitive demographics.
privacy_classes.handling_notes: Restrict by least privilege and audit access.

privacy_classes.plain_description: Achievement or learner-record data intended to be verified or shared under learner/institution control.
privacy_classes.handling_notes: Respect issuer, learner, verification, revocation, and sharing rules.

privacy_classes.plain_description: Activity or usage data, such as clicks, sessions, time-on-task, and resource use.
privacy_classes.handling_notes: Prefer aggregation for analytics; restrict raw event access.

privacy_classes.plain_description: System records about imports, exports, validation, lineage, and operations.
privacy_classes.handling_notes: Useful for debugging; may reveal partner or tenant behavior.

privacy_classes.plain_description: Internal platform configuration or infrastructure metadata.
privacy_classes.handling_notes: Do not expose through public APIs unless explicitly designed for it.

domains.name: Tenancy and Governance
domains.plain_description: The rules and boundaries that say which school, provider, or app owns and may access data.

domains.name: Identity and Organizations
domains.plain_description: People, schools, districts, departments, roles, and the IDs other systems use for them.

domains.name: Academic Structure
domains.plain_description: The school calendar, courses, classes, sections, and enrollments that describe where learning happens.

domains.name: Curriculum and Standards
domains.plain_description: Academic standards, competencies, skills, rubrics, and the links between them.

domains.name: Assessment Content
domains.plain_description: Questions, tests, assessment packages, scoring metadata, and accessibility metadata.

domains.name: Outcomes and Gradebook
domains.plain_description: Assignments, gradebook columns, score scales, attempts, and results.

domains.name: Learning Activity
domains.plain_description: Events that describe what learners and educators did in tools and content.

domains.name: Tool Integration
domains.plain_description: Registrations, launches, deep links, memberships, and grade passback for third-party tools.

domains.name: Credentials
domains.plain_description: Badges, achievements, learner records, endorsements, and verification status.

domains.name: Operational Metadata
domains.plain_description: Imports, exports, validation results, source systems, and lineage records.

objects.name: Organization
objects.plain_description: A school, district, department, program, or other education organization.
objects.why_it_exists: Apps need to know where people, courses, and classes belong.

fields.plain_description: The platform's own stable ID for this organization.
fields.school_example: The platform ID for Lincoln Middle School.
fields.access_notes: Visible to authorized tenant users and apps with organization scopes.
fields.common_mistakes: Do not treat this as the SIS or state organization ID.

fields.plain_description: The name people use for the organization.
fields.access_notes: Visible to authorized users who can see the organization.

fields.plain_description: The kind of organization this is.
fields.access_notes: Visible with organization read access.

allowed_values.label: District
allowed_values.plain_description: A school district or local education agency.

allowed_values.label: Department
allowed_values.plain_description: A department or division inside a school or institution.

allowed_values.label: Program
allowed_values.plain_description: A program, academy, pathway, or other organized learning group.

objects.name: Person
objects.plain_description: A learner, teacher, staff member, guardian, or other person known to the platform.
objects.why_it_exists: Schools and tools need a consistent way to refer to people while respecting privacy.

fields.plain_description: The platform's own stable ID for this person.
fields.school_example: The platform ID for a specific student.
fields.access_notes: Only visible to authorized tenant users and scoped applications.

fields.plain_description: The name shown in user interfaces when the person is displayed.
fields.access_notes: May be limited by district directory policy.

fields.plain_description: The person's broad role in the education environment.
fields.access_notes: Visible to apps with appropriate roster or launch context.

allowed_values.label: Student
allowed_values.plain_description: A learner enrolled in one or more classes.

allowed_values.label: Teacher
allowed_values.plain_description: An educator assigned to teach or manage learning activities.

allowed_values.label: Administrator
allowed_values.plain_description: A school or district user with administrative responsibilities.

allowed_values.label: Guardian
allowed_values.plain_description: A parent, guardian, or other adult connected to a learner.

objects.name: Enrollment
objects.plain_description: A record that says a person participates in a class with a specific role.
objects.why_it_exists: Apps need to know which learners and teachers are in each class.

fields.plain_description: The person who is enrolled in the class.
fields.access_notes: Visible only when the requester may see that class roster.

fields.plain_description: The class where the person is enrolled.
fields.access_notes: Visible only when the requester may see the class.

fields.plain_description: The person's job in the class.
fields.access_notes: Visible through roster APIs and LTI launch context when scoped.
fields.common_mistakes: A person's class role can differ from their primary account role.

objects.name: CASE Item
objects.plain_description: One standard, competency, skill, learning objective, course code, or framework item.
objects.why_it_exists: Content, assessments, grades, and credentials need to point to the same official learning goal.

fields.plain_description: The official CASE unique identifier for this standard or competency item.
fields.access_notes: Generally public if the framework is public.
fields.common_mistakes: Do not replace the CASE GUID with the short printed standard code.

fields.plain_description: The short code people see in curriculum documents.
fields.access_notes: Generally public if the framework is public.

fields.plain_description: The text that describes what the learner should know or be able to do.
fields.school_example: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly.
fields.access_notes: Generally public if the framework is public.

objects.name: QTI Package
objects.plain_description: A packaged assessment, test, question bank, or item set that follows QTI rules.
objects.why_it_exists: Assessment content must move between authoring tools, item banks, delivery systems, and reporting systems.

fields.plain_description: The identifier supplied inside the QTI package.
fields.school_example: The publisher's package ID for a Grade 6 reading assessment.
fields.access_notes: Visible to assessment managers and authorized assessment tools.

fields.plain_description: The QTI version the package claims to follow.
fields.access_notes: Visible with assessment package read access.

fields.plain_description: Whether the platform has checked the package and found it valid, invalid, or not yet checked.
fields.access_notes: Visible to assessment managers and integration operators.

objects.name: Caliper Event
objects.plain_description: A record that something happened in a learning tool, such as a launch, view, submission, or grade action.
objects.why_it_exists: Schools and providers need consistent learning activity data for analytics and product improvement.

fields.plain_description: The unique event ID from the tool or from the platform ingestion process.
fields.school_example: The ID for one student opening a reading assignment.
fields.access_notes: Raw events are restricted; most apps should use aggregates.

fields.plain_description: The Caliper profile that explains what kind of learning activity this event describes.
fields.access_notes: Visible only with learning activity access.

fields.plain_description: What happened in the learning tool.
fields.school_example: started, completed, submitted, graded, viewed, or searched
fields.access_notes: Visible only with learning activity access.

objects.name: Source Identifier
objects.plain_description: An ID used by another system to refer to the same thing the platform tracks.
objects.why_it_exists: Schools and vendors need to match records across SIS, LMS, assessment, standards, and credential systems.

fields.plain_description: The outside system that created or owns the identifier.
fields.school_example: PowerSchool SIS, Canvas LMS, a state standards publisher, or a test vendor.
fields.access_notes: Visible to integration operators and authorized support users.

fields.plain_description: The identifier value used by the outside system.
fields.school_example: A OneRoster sourcedId or CASE GUID.
fields.access_notes: Access depends on the object being identified.
fields.common_mistakes: External IDs are not globally unique unless paired with their source system and identifier type.

fields.plain_description: The kind of outside ID this is.
fields.school_example: oneroster_sourced_id, case_guid, lti_deployment_id, qti_identifier, or open_badge_credential_id.
fields.access_notes: Visible to integration operators and authorized support users.

relationships.plain_description: Each enrollment names the person who participates in the class.

relationships.cardinality: many packages can align to many CASE items
relationships.plain_description: Assessment content can be linked to the standards or competencies it measures.

relationships.cardinality: many events can reference one person
relationships.plain_description: A learning activity event may identify the learner or educator who performed the action.
